message:
feat(db,tools,server): add reaper_exempt flag to AgentRow for long-lived agents

Add a new `reaper_exempt` column (default 0) to AgentRow so that
specific agents can be marked as exempt from the inactivity reaper.
This prevents the cleanup subsystem from releasing reservations or
treating permanently-running agents (e.g. orchestrators, supervisors)
as stale when they haven't sent messages recently.

Changes span the full call chain:

- db/models.rs: new `reaper_exempt: i64` field with sqlmodel default 0
- tools/identity.rs: `register_agent` accepts `reaper_exempt: Option<bool>`,
  threads it to the DB layer, and surfaces it in AgentResponse
- tools/macros.rs: `macro_start_session` accepts and forwards reaper_exempt;
  `macro_prepare_thread` passes None (normal agents)
- tools/contacts.rs, messaging.rs, reservations.rs, resources.rs: all
  call-sites updated with the new parameter (None for existing callers)
- server/cleanup.rs: `detect_and_release_stale` now checks
  `agent.reaper_exempt != 0` and skips exempt agents entirely
- server/ack_ttl.rs, mail_ui.rs, tui_app.rs: test and production
  call-sites updated for the new register_agent signature
- cli/lib.rs: CLI agent registration paths pass None
- All 17 DB test files, storage tests, and benchmarks updated to pass
  the additional None parameter to register_agent calls

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
